metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1329
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors generated a calcineurin B knock out mice. The loss of CNB1 induced the loss of CNA subunit and therefore calcineurin activity in CA1 and DG regions of the hippocampus but not CA3 neurons. Authors focussed in the Schaffer collateral-CA1 synapse in hippocampal slices to investigate the electrophysiological properties of neurons lacking CNB1, which in this model and in this synapse is absent only in the postsynaptic compartment. LTD induced with low-frequency stimulation was significantly reduced in the mutant mice. When studying plasticity in conditions where LTP and LTD compete, mutant mice showed a preference for LTP over LTD. Interestingly, the same LTD protocol that failed to induce LTD in mutant mice was able to reverse LTP when delivered 30 min after LTP induction.

17/11/2017 Pim
- The original selected term was regulation of 'postsynaptic process involved in chemical synaptic transmission (GO:0099565)'. This s correct especially considering that the genetic perturbation was only conducted in the postsynaptic cell
- I have changed the term to 'postsynaptic modulation of chemical synaptic transmission (GO:0099170)'. Experimental description: From M&M section:
Experiments were performed in hippocampal slices from WT and mutant mice.
Mutant mice were generated using (literal): "a fCNB1-targeting vector was constructed such that the three major coding exons, E2, E3, and E4, were flanked by a loxP site and an LFNT cassette".
